verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have manifested Thy Name unto the men which Thou gavest Me out of the world; Thine they were, and Thou gavest them Me, and they have kept Thy word.”

verses.text: 6 , LXX.), the Son of God is called, because of the other things which He taught, and principally because He announced the Father to men, as also now He saith, “I have manifested Thy Name unto the men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For after having said, “I have finished Thy work,” He next explaineth it in detail, telling what sort of work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the Name indeed was well known.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Esaias said, “Ye shall swear by the true God.” ( Isa. lxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what I have often told you I tell you now, that though it was known, yet it was so only to Jews, and not to all of these: but now He speaketh concerning the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor doth He declare this merely, but also that they knew Him as the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is not the same thing to learn that He is Creator, and that He hath a Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He “manifested His Name” both by words and actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whom Thou gavest Me out of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As He saith above, “No man cometh unto Me except it be given him” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 65 ); and, “Except My Father draw him” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 64 ); so here too, “Whom thou gavest Me.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now He calleth Himself “the Way”; whence it is clear that He establisheth two things by what is said here, that He is not opposed to the Father, and that it is the Father’s will to entrust them to the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thine they were, and Thou gavest them Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He desireth to teach that He is greatly loved by the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that He needed not to receive them, is clear from this, He made them, He careth for them continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did He receive them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This, as I said before, showeth His unanimity with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if a man choose to enquire into the matter in a human manner, and as the words are spoken, they will no longer belong to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if when the Father had them, the Son had them not, it is evident that when He gave them to the Son, He withdrew from His dominion over them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, there is a yet more unseemly conclusion; for they will be found to have been imperfect while they yet were with the Father, but to have become perfect when they came to the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it is mockery even to speak thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth He declare by this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That it hath seemed good to the Father also that they should believe on the Son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And they have kept Thy word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now they have known that all things whatsoever Thou hast given Me are of Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How did they “keep Thy word”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “By believing in Me, and giving no heed to the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that believeth in Him, it saith, ‘hath set to his seal that God is true.’” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some read, “Now I know that all things whatsoever Thou hast given Me are of Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this would have no reason; for how would the Son be ignorant of the things of the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No the words are spoken of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “From the time,” He saith, “that I told them these things, they have learnt that all that Thou hast given Me is from Thee; nothing is alien, nothing peculiar to Me, with Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (For whatever is peculiar, puts most things in the condition of being alien.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They therefore have known that all things, whatsoever I teach, are Thy doctrines and teachings.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whence have they learnt it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From My words; for so have I taught them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not only this have I taught them, but also that “I came out from Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this He was anxious to prove through all the Gospel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I pray for them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sayest Thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Dost Thou teach the Father, as though He were ignorant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost Thou speak to Him as to a man who knoweth not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then meaneth this distinction?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the prayer is for nothing else than that they may understand the love which He hath towards them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He who not only giveth what He hath of His own, but also calleth on Another to do the same, showeth greater love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is, “I pray for them”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Not for all the world,” He saith, but “for them whom Thou hast given Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He continually putteth the “hast given,” that they might learn that this seemeth good to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, because He had said continually, “they are Thine,” and, “Thou gavest them unto Me,” to remove any evil suspicion, and lest any one should think that His authority was recent, and that He had but now received them, what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “All Mine are Thine, and Thine are Mine; and I am glorified in them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the equality of honor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For lest on hearing, “Thou hast given them Me,” thou shouldest deem that they were alienated from the authority of the Father, or before this from that of the Son, He removed both difficulties by speaking as He did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was as though He said, “Do not when thou hearest that ‘Thou hast given them to Me,’ deem that they are alienated from the Father, for what is Mine is His; nor when thou hearest, ‘Thine they were,’ think that they were aliens from Me, for what is His is Mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that the, “Thou hast given,” is said only for condescension; for what the Father hath is the Son’s, and what the Son hath is the Father’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this cannot even be said of a son after the manner of man, but because They are upon a greater Equality of honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that what belongs to the less, belongs to the greater also, is clear to every one, but the reverse not so; but here He converteth these terms, and the conversion declares Equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in another place, declaring this, He said, “All things that the Father hath are Mine,” speaking of knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the “hast given Me,” and the like expressions, are to show that He did not come as an alien and draw them to Him, but received them as His own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He putteth the cause and the proof, saying, “And I am glorified in them,” that is, either that “I have power over them,” or, that “they shall glorify Me, believing in Thee and Me, and shall glorify Us alike.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if He is not glorified equally in them, what is the Father’s is no longer His.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For no one is glorified in those over whom he hath no authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet how is He glorified equally?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All die for Him equally as for the Father; they preach Him as they do the Father; and as they say that all things are done in His Name, so also in the Name of the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “Although I appear no longer in the flesh, yet by these am I glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why doth He say continuously, that, “I am not in the world”; and that, “because I leave them I commit them to Thee”; and that, “when I was in the world I kept them”? for if one should take these words in their simple sense, many absurdities will follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how could it be reasonable to say, that He is no longer in the world, and that when He departeth He committeth them to another? since these are the words of a mere man parting from them forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He speaketh for the most part like a man, and in a way adapted to their state of mind, because they thought that they had a greater degree of safety from His presence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “While I was with them, I kept them.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He telleth them, “I come to you”; and, “I am with you till the end.” ( Matt. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith He these words, as if about to be parted from them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He addresseth Himself, as I said before, to their thoughts, that they may take breath a little when they hear Him speaking thus, and delivering them over to the care of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since, after hearing many exhortations from Him, they were not persuaded, He then holdeth converse with the Father, manifesting His affection for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Since Thou callest Me to Thyself, place these in safety; for I come to Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sayest Thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Art Thou not able to keep them?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yea, I am able.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wherefore then speakest Thou thus?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That they may have My joy fulfilled” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ); that is, “may not be confounded, as being imperfect.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by these words He showed that He had spoken all these things so, to give them rest and joy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the saying appears to be contradictory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now I am no longer in the world, and these are in the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This was what they were suspecting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a while therefore He condescendeth to them, because had He said, “I keep them,” they would not have so well believed; wherefore He saith, “Holy Father, keep them through Thine own Name”; that is, “by thy help.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “While I was with them in the world, I kept them in Thy Name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He speaketh as a man and as a Prophet, since nowhere doth He appear to have done anything by the Name of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Those that Thou gavest Me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in another place He saith, “Of all that Thou gavest Me, I will surely lose nothing.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not only was he lost, but also many afterwards; how then saith He, “I will in nowise lose”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For My part, I will not lose.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So in another place, declaring the matter was more clearly, He said, “I will in nowise cast out.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Not through fault of Mine, not because I either instigate or abandon them; but if they start away of themselves, I draw them not by necessity.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But now I come to thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the discourse is composed rather in a human manner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that should any wish from these words to lower the Son, he will lower the Father also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, in proof of this, how from the beginning He speaketh partly as though informing and explaining to Him, partly as enjoining.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Informing, as when He saith, “I pray not for the world”; enjoining, as, “I have kept them until now,” “and none of them is lost”; and, “do Thou therefore now keep them,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “Thine they were, and Thou hast given them unto Me” and “While I was in the world I kept them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the solution of all is, that the words were addressed to their infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But after having said that “none of them was lost but the son of perdition,” He added, “that the Scripture might be fulfilled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of what Scripture doth He speak?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That which foretelleth many things concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not that He perished on that account, in order that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we have before spoken at length on this point, that this is the peculiar manner of Scripture, which puts things which fall out in accordance with it, as though they were caused by it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it is needful to enquire exactly into all, both the manner of the speaker, his argument, and the laws of Scripture, if at least we are minded not to draw wrong conclusions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “Brethren, be not children in your minds.” ( 1 Cor. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This it is necessary to consider well, not only for the understanding the Scriptures, but also for earnestness in one’s way of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so little children do not desire great things, but are wont to admire those which are worth nothing; they are pleased at seeing chariots, and horses, and the muleteer, and wheels, all made out of earthenware; but if they see a king sitting upon a chariot, and a pair of white mules, and great magnificence, they do not even turn their heads.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they deck out as brides dolls made of the same material, but the actual brides, real and beautiful, they do not even notice; and this is their case in many other matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now this many men also undergo at this time; for when they hear of heavenly things, they do not even give heed to them, but toward all the things of clay they are as eager as children, and stupidly admire the wealth which is of earth, and honor the glory and luxury of the present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet these are just as much toys as those; but the other are the causes of life, and glory, and repose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But as children deprived of their playthings cry, and do not know how even to desire the realities, so also are many of those who seem to be men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it saith, “Be not children in your minds.” ( 1 Cor. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Desirest thou riches, tell me, and desirest thou not the wealth that lasteth, but childish toys?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou shouldest see a man admiring a leaden coin, and stooping to pick it up, thou wouldest pronounce his penury to be extreme; and dost thou, who collectest more worthless things than this, number thyself among the rich?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How can this consist with reason?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We will call him rich who despises all present things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For no one, no one will choose to laugh at these little things, silver and gold, and other things of show, unless he have the desire of greater things; just as the man would not despise the leaden coin, unless he possessed coins of gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do thou, therefore, when thou seest a man running by all worldly things, deem that he doth so from no other motive than because he looks to a greater world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So the husbandman despises a few grains of wheat, when he expects a larger harvest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if, when the hope is uncertain, we despise things which are, much more ought we to do so in a case where the expectation is sure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I pray and beseech you not to bring loss on yourselves, nor, keeping hold of mire, rob yourselves of the treasures which are above, bringing your vessel to port laden with straw and chaff.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let each say what he will concerning us, let him be angry at our continual admonitions, let him call us silly, tedious, tiresome, still we will not desist from exhorting you on these matters continually, and from continually repeating to you that of the Prophet, “‘Break off thy sins by almsgiving, and thine iniquities by showing mercy to the poor’ ( Dan. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ), and bind them upon thy neck.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do not act in this way to-day, and desist to-morrow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even this body has need of daily food; and so too hath the soul, or rather that much more; and if it give not, it becomes weaker and more vile.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then not neglect it when it is perishing, choking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many wounds it receives each day, by being lustful, angry, slothful, reviling, revengeful, envious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is therefore necessary to prepare also remedies for it, and no small remedy is that of almsgiving, which can be placed on every wound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “Give alms,” it saith, “of such things as ye have, and behold all things are clean unto you.” ( Luke xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Alms,” not covetousness, for that which proceeds from covetousness endures not, though thou give to those who need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For almsgiving is that which is free from all injustice, “this” makes all things clean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is a thing better even than fasting, or lying on the ground; they may be more painful and laborious, but this more profitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It enlightens the soul, makes it sleek, beautiful, and vigorous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not so doth the fruit of the olive hold up the athletes, as this oil recovers the combatants of piety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then anoint our hands, that we may lift them up well against our adversary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that practiceth showing mercy to him that needeth, will soon cease from covetousness, he who continues in giving to the poor, will soon cease from anger, and will never even be high-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the physician continually tending wounded persons is easily sobered, beholding human nature in the calamities of others; so we, if we enter upon the work of aiding the poor, shall easily become truly wise, and shall not admire riches, nor deem present things any great matter, but despise them all, and soaring aloft to heaven, shall easily obtain the eternal blessings, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ; to whom, with the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory for ever and ever.
